title: Fair Play for Girls Act
plain_summary: The Fair Play for Girls Act is a law that tells the Attorney General to study violence against women and girls who play sports. The Attorney General must write a report within one year that looks at the different problems female athletes face with violence. The report also needs to include ideas for how to fix these problems and make sports safer for women and girls. This report will be given to Congress so they can understand what is happening and what can be done to help protect female athletes.
last_action_text: Read twice and referred to the Committee on the Judiciary.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Fair Play for Girls Act.

sections.title: Section 2 Report
sections.text: Within one year after this law is passed, the Attorney General must write a report about violence against women in sports in the United States. The Attorney General must send this report to six different committees in Congress. Three committees are in the Senate and three are in the House of Representatives. The report must include two main parts. First, the report must analyze several things. It must look at what makes it hard for female athletes who were born female to have fair and safe competition. It must study how often female athletes who were born female lose opportunities like medals and championships when they compete against people who were born male. It must check how well state laws work when they try to reduce the risk of physical harm and lost opportunities that happen when biological men are allowed to compete in women's sports. It must examine how often women and girls in sports face online violence, harassment, and abuse, and what causes these problems. It must study how often women and girls in sports experience sexual harassment and abuse. It must also check how well federal and state laws work to prevent sexual harassment and abuse of women and girls in sports. Second, the report must give policy recommendations to solve all the problems described in the first part.
